quiz.title: Unit 4 Quiz 10, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Molecular Shapes and Polarity
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Molecular Geometry, VSEPR Theory, Polarity of Molecules, Symmetry and Dipole Moments, Electron Configurations of Ions

questions.question: The electronic configuration 2, 8, 18, 32, 18, 8 belongs to:
questions.explanation: The configuration 2, 8, 18, 32, 18, 8 totals 86 electrons, which is the atomic number of radon (Rn). Radon is the heaviest noble gas and has this electron configuration by shells.

questions.question: What is the electronic configuration for Sodium ion Na⁺?
questions.explanation: Sodium atom (Na) has configuration 2, 8, 1. When it loses one electron to form Na⁺, it loses its valence electron, leaving configuration 2, 8 — the same as neon.

questions.question: Which molecule has polar bonds but is non-polar overall?
questions.explanation: CO₂ has polar C=O bonds, but the molecule is linear and symmetric, so the bond dipoles cancel each other out, making it non-polar overall. H₂O, NH₃, and HCl all have net dipole moments.

questions.question: Which molecule has a net dipole moment?
questions.explanation: NH₃ (ammonia) has a trigonal pyramidal shape with a lone pair on nitrogen, creating an uneven charge distribution and a net dipole moment. CO₂ and BF₃ are symmetric and non-polar, and CCl₄ is tetrahedral and symmetric, making it non-polar as well.

questions.question: Which shape usually leads to a non-polar molecule (if atoms are identical)?
questions.explanation: Linear symmetric molecules (like CO₂ or BeCl₂) have bond dipoles that cancel out, making them non-polar if the terminal atoms are identical. Bent and trigonal pyramidal shapes are usually polar, and asymmetric shapes are polar by definition.

questions.question: Which molecule is non-polar due to its tetrahedral symmetry?
questions.explanation: CCl₄ (carbon tetrachloride) has a tetrahedral shape with four identical chlorine atoms. The symmetry causes all bond dipoles to cancel, making it non-polar. NH₃ and H₂O are polar, and CH₃Cl has an asymmetric tetrahedral shape with a net dipole.

questions.question: Which molecule is polar because of its bent shape?
questions.explanation: SO₂ (sulfur dioxide) has a bent shape due to a lone pair on sulfur, creating an uneven charge distribution and a net dipole moment. CO₂ is linear and non-polar, BF₃ is trigonal planar and non-polar, and CH₄ is tetrahedral and non-polar.

questions.question: A molecule with a trigonal pyramidal shape is usually:
questions.explanation: Trigonal pyramidal molecules (like NH₃) are usually polar because the lone pair at the apex creates an uneven distribution of charge. The bond dipoles do not cancel out due to the asymmetric shape.

questions.question: Which statement is TRUE?
options: Molecules with polar bonds are always polar
questions.explanation: Symmetry can cancel bond polarity, making a molecule non-polar even if it has polar bonds (e.g., CO₂, CCl₄). Molecules with polar bonds are not always polar (A is false), shape definitely affects polarity (B is false), and not all covalent compounds are polar (D is false).

questions.question: Which type of molecule is most likely to be polar?
questions.explanation: Bent or pyramidal molecules are most likely to be polar because their asymmetric shapes prevent bond dipoles from canceling out. Symmetric molecules (linear, tetrahedral with identical atoms) are non-polar, and diatomic molecules with identical atoms are non-polar.

questions.question: What is the molecular geometry of the methane (CH₄) molecule?
questions.explanation: Methane (CH₄) has a tetrahedral shape with bond angles of approximately 109.5°. Carbon forms four single bonds with no lone pairs, giving this symmetric geometry.

questions.question: The molecular geometry of a carbon dioxide (CO₂) molecule is:
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide (CO₂) has a linear shape with bond angles of 180°. Carbon forms two double bonds with oxygen atoms and has no lone pairs, resulting in this linear geometry.

questions.question: What is the molecular geometry of water (H₂O)?
questions.explanation: Water (H₂O) has a bent shape with a bond angle of about 104.5°. Oxygen has two lone pairs that repel the bonding pairs, compressing the angle from the ideal tetrahedral 109.5°.

questions.question: The molecular geometry of ammonia (NH₃) is trigonal pyramidal because:
options: It has one lone pair on nitrogen
It has two lone pairs on nitrogen
questions.explanation: Ammonia has a trigonal pyramidal shape because nitrogen has one lone pair. The lone pair repels the three bonding pairs, pushing them into a pyramid shape with bond angles of about 107°.

questions.question: Which molecule has a trigonal planar molecular geometry?
questions.explanation: BF₃ (boron trifluoride) has a trigonal planar shape with bond angles of 120°. Boron has three bonding pairs and no lone pairs, giving this flat, symmetric geometry.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered molecular shapes and polarity! 🧪📐
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review how symmetry affects molecular polarity.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on VSEPR shapes and identifying polar vs non-polar molecules.
scoreMessages.0: Please review molecular geometry, VSEPR theory, and factors that determine polarity.
